svg.text: • Network design: minimum-cost cabling, fiber backbone, road network planning.
• Cluster analysis: single-linkage clustering = build MST, cut at threshold.
• Approximation algorithms: 2-approx for metric TSP via MST traversal.
• VLSI / chip routing: minimum-length signal nets, clock-tree synthesis.
• Phylogenetic trees: initial topology for distance-based methods.
• Network reliability: spanning trees as backbone for fault-tolerant routing.
• Maximum spanning tree: sort descending. Useful in correlation graphs.
• k-MST / minimum bottleneck spanning tree: bounded-edge-count or min-max-edge variants.
• Steiner tree: spanning subset of vertices, NP-hard in general.
• Degree-constrained MST: NP-hard; LP relaxation + rounding gives approx.
• Online / dynamic MST: Holm-Lichtenberg-Thorup, polylog amortized per update.
• Distributed MST: GHS (Gallager-Humblet-Spira) algorithm, O(V log V) messages.
MST family extends from a 5-line greedy to deep distributed and online algorithms.
